name: Kingdom of Hawaii (1845–1893)

colors.symbolism: red stripes — the eight major islands

description: Eight horizontal stripes alternating white, red and blue (in repeating order, top to bottom: white, red, blue, white, red, blue, white, red), with the British Union Flag in the upper hoist canton.
history: The flag of the Kingdom of Hawaii was adopted under King Kamehameha I in 1816 (with various proportional refinements through the 19th century). The eight stripes represent the eight main Hawaiian islands. The Union Flag in the canton honours the British protection extended to the islands. After the overthrow of the monarchy in 1893 the flag continued in use under the Provisional Government, the Republic, the Territory and finally the State of Hawaii — the only US state flag bearing the Union Jack.

features.symbolism: the eight main islands of Hawaii
